methodology: Platform-product OKRs with customer outcomes and explicit team ownership.
description: Archived quarterly Objectives covering the prior two years before the current quarter.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe internal-customer and engineering-system outcomes, not platform feature shipping alone.
Key results are grouped under a small number of company objectives.
Every key result has explicit team ownership.

companyObjectives.title: Archive Establish a usable shared engineering foundation
companyObjectives.statement: Focus archived periods on making shared runtime, delivery, and onboarding capabilities reliable enough for broad platform adoption.

companyObjectives.title: Archive Raise the safety and operability baseline
companyObjectives.statement: Focus archived periods on embedding guardrails, telemetry, and incident discipline into the standard path rather than relying on manual governance and heroics.

objectives.title: 2025-Q4 Establish a credible onboarding paved road
objectives.objective: Make the platform front door credible enough that mainstream teams can start on shared defaults instead of building local setup paths from scratch.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Measures how quickly teams become productive on the platform.
Share of onboarding and setup flows completed without platform intervention.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of mainstream product teams using the standard platform path.

keyResults.target: Make first-team platform startup materially faster than bespoke setup
keyResults.baseline: Teams previously treated onboarding as a multi-week coordination exercise.
keyResults.statement: Reduce onboarding time by introducing a clearer platform front door, stronger templates, and more standard ownership metadata.

executingTeams.intent: Led the creation of a credible portal and catalog entry point for mainstream teams.

supportingTeams.intent: Supported the KR with first-generation templates and supported service patterns.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This archived KR reflects an outcome the team achieved through better product quality rather than feature volume alone.

target1.label: 2026-Q1 target
target1.target: Make first-team platform startup materially faster than bespoke setup
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Median team onboarding time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward make first-team platform startup materially faster than bespoke setup, driven by reduce onboarding time by introducing a clearer platform front door, stronger templates, and more standard ownership metadata.

target2.label: 2026-Q2 target
target2.target: Make first-team platform startup materially faster than bespoke setup
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Median team onboarding time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with make first-team platform startup materially faster than bespoke setup holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Establish enough credibility that more teams choose the standard path
keyResults.baseline: Adoption was previously blocked by weak defaults and too much hidden setup work.
keyResults.statement: Increase platform adoption by making onboarding and service setup more consistent for mainstream teams.

executingTeams.intent: Led the effort to turn platform onboarding into a repeatable starting point.

supportingTeams.intent: Supported the KR through migration help and feedback loops from early adopting teams.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This archived KR captures whether the onboarding foundation actually translated into real team adoption.

target1.label: 2026-Q1 target
target1.target: Establish enough credibility that more teams choose the standard path
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Platform adoption across product teams moves far enough to show clear momentum toward establish enough credibility that more teams choose the standard path, driven by increase platform adoption by making onboarding and service setup more consistent for mainstream teams.

target2.label: 2026-Q2 target
target2.target: Establish enough credibility that more teams choose the standard path
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Platform adoption across product teams ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with establish enough credibility that more teams choose the standard path holding across the broader operating model.

objectives.title: 2025-Q4 Standardize the runtime delivery path
objectives.objective: Create a shared runtime and delivery baseline that reduces local pipeline ownership and makes the standard path usable for production services.

linkedCustomerKPIs.kpiName: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Elapsed time from a new service starting to its first production release on the platform.
Engineering effort spent keeping custom delivery pipelines working outside the platform standard path.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Time required for a code change to reach production through the platform path.

keyResults.metric: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
keyResults.target: Make the default runtime path usable for real delivery work
keyResults.baseline: Teams still lost time stitching together runtime, environment, and release primitives.
keyResults.statement: Reduce repo-to-production time by packaging more of the runtime and release path into reusable platform defaults.

executingTeams.intent: Led the work to make environment creation and readiness more standard.

supportingTeams.intent: Supported the KR through reusable release automation and promotion defaults.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This archived KR reflects the first credible standard runtime and delivery path becoming available.

target1.label: 2026-Q1 target
target1.target: Make the default runtime path usable for real delivery work
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Time from repo creation to first production deployment moves far enough to show clear momentum toward make the default runtime path usable for real delivery work, driven by reduce repo-to-production time by packaging more of the runtime and release path into reusable platform defaults.

target2.label: 2026-Q2 target
target2.target: Make the default runtime path usable for real delivery work
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Time from repo creation to first production deployment ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with make the default runtime path usable for real delivery work holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Lower the waiting time introduced by delivery and runtime setup work
keyResults.baseline: Shared delivery foundations were still too incomplete and inconsistent to reduce flow reliably.
keyResults.statement: Reduce lead time by making the shared delivery path simpler, more repeatable, and less dependent on local repair work.

executingTeams.intent: Led the work to turn deployment and release mechanics into reusable platform workflows.

supportingTeams.intent: Supported the KR by reducing the dependency setup friction that slowed real delivery work.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This archived KR captures the business effect of making the shared runtime path usable.

target1.label: 2026-Q1 target
target1.target: Lower the waiting time introduced by delivery and runtime setup work
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Lead time for change moves far enough to show clear momentum toward lower the waiting time introduced by delivery and runtime setup work, driven by reduce lead time by making the shared delivery path simpler, more repeatable, and less dependent on local repair work.

target2.label: 2026-Q2 target
target2.target: Lower the waiting time introduced by delivery and runtime setup work
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Lead time for change ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with lower the waiting time introduced by delivery and runtime setup work holding across the broader operating model.
